evidence.evidence_text: tion: NHL 3.5 (Binder A), NHL 3.5 Z (Binder B) and NHL 2 (Binder C). The binder D is a pre-formulated lime-based binder made up of air lime (75%), hydraulic lime (15%) and pozzolanic lime (10%). The bulk densities of the various binders are shown in Table 2 . 2.1.2. Mechanical behavior Specimens of pure binder were manufactured using binder D and compressive strength measurements were made after 14 days, 2 months, 6 months, 12 months and 15 months of setting ( Fig. 1 ). The mechanical behavior of the pure binder changes with time: the gradual hydration of lime during the setting allows a progres- sive improvement of the mechanical strength and of the stiffness of material and also leads to a reduction of the strain at failure. The mechanical strengths measured are lower than those of a pure Portland cement with a high rate of clinker (CEM I) which are higher than 30 MPa after 28 days of setting.
